output.dependency: The values describe product package size and a per-unit measurement. The per-unit measurement value (a decimal string) is consistent with being derived from or used alongside the package size (e.g., nutrition/strength per ounce vs total based on container size).

The decimal measurement (string form) and the numeric measurement are consistent representations of the same underlying quantity (e.g., 0.065 corresponds to 65.0 after scaling such as ×1000), indicating a deterministic formatting/scaling relationship.

One numeric field represents a quantity that appears to be derived from another numeric field via a scaling factor (e.g., the larger integer is consistent with the other measurement expressed in different units or resolution).
The location fields are semantically linked: a city/locality value is associated with a broader region/state code. The locality value determines which region/state code it belongs to.
The product/brand name is semantically dependent on the producer/manufacturer field: the producer value indicates the brewery that makes the named beer.
The style/classification field is semantically dependent on the product name: the named beer implies a specific style/category (e.g., the same beer maps consistently to the same style text).
